items.question_text: What action must be taken when a CA or MCI alert is displayed?
items.explanation: 5-13-1a requires evaluating the reason for the alert without delay and taking appropriate action.

choices.text: Evaluate the reason for the alert without delay and take appropriate action
Acknowledge the alert and continue monitoring
Immediately issue a vector to both aircraft
Suppress the alert and continue normal operations

items.explanation: E-MSAW stands for En Route Minimum Safe Altitude Warning.

choices.text: En Route Minimum Safe Altitude Warning
Emergency Minimum Sector Altitude Warning
En route Mode C Safety Alert Warning
Extended Minimum Safe Approach Warning

items.question_text: When E-MSAW alert suppression is used indefinitely, for what type of operations would it typically apply?
items.explanation: 5-13-2 note states indefinite suppression typically applies to military flights with clearance to fly low-level routes below established minimum IFR altitudes.

choices.text: Military flights flying low-level routes below minimum IFR altitudes
All VFR aircraft
Aircraft on visual approaches
Aircraft in holding patterns

items.question_text: What must the altitude field(s) of the data block always reflect?
items.explanation: 5-13-3a1 requires the altitude field(s) to always reflect the current status of the aircraft unless otherwise specified in a facility directive.

choices.text: The current status of the aircraft
The filed flight plan altitude
The last assigned altitude
The sector altitude

items.question_text: When must flight plan route data NOT be modified?
items.explanation: 5-13-3b states flight plan route data must not be modified outside the controller's area of jurisdiction unless verbally coordinated or specified in an LOA or facility directive.

choices.text: Outside the controller's area of jurisdiction without verbal coordination or LOA/facility directive
At any time
Only during emergencies
Only when the aircraft is in holding

items.question_text: When must reported altitudes be entered into the computer?
items.explanation: 5-13-4 requires entry: (a) when aircraft reaches assigned altitude, (b) when issued clearance to climb/descend, (c) minimum each 10,000 feet during climb/descent from FL180 and above.

choices.text: When reaching assigned altitude, when issued climb/descent clearance, and each 10,000 ft during climb/descent from FL180+
Only when requested by the supervisor
Only when Mode C is unavailable
Only at initial contact

items.question_text: For Merging Target Procedures, what altitude stratum must sectors display beyond their own sector?
items.explanation: 5-13-5 requires 1,200 feet above/below where 1,000 ft vertical separation applies, and 2,200 feet above/below where 2,000 ft vertical separation applies.

choices.text: 1,200 ft above/below for 1,000 ft sep; 2,200 ft above/below for 2,000 ft sep
1,000 ft above and below sector limits
5,000 ft above and below sector limits
Only the sector's own altitude stratum

items.question_text: When may the OK function be used to override sector eligibility?
items.explanation: 5-13-6 permits OK function override only when: (a) prior coordination is effected, or (b) the flight is within the control jurisdiction of the sector.

choices.text: When prior coordination is effected or the flight is within the sector's control jurisdiction
At any time the controller deems necessary
Only with supervisor approval
Only during emergencies

items.question_text: May coast tracks be used for separation?
items.explanation: 5-13-7 explicitly prohibits using coast tracks in the application of either radar or nonradar separation criteria.

choices.text: No, they may not be used for radar or nonradar separation
Yes, for radar separation only
Yes, for nonradar separation only
Yes, for both radar and nonradar separation

items.question_text: In what mode must coast tracks be initiated?
items.explanation: 5-13-8a requires initiating coast tracks only in Flight Plan Aided Tracking (FLAT) mode, except free coast tracking may be used as a reminder for non-flight-plan aircraft.

choices.text: Flight Plan Aided Tracking (FLAT) mode only
Any tracking mode available
Free coast tracking mode only
Radar tracking mode only

items.question_text: When must a hold message be entered into ERAM?
items.explanation: 5-13-9a requires entering a hold message when aircraft is issued holding instructions, ATC initiates the delay, and EFC is other than 'no delay expected.'

choices.text: When holding is ATC-initiated and EFC is other than 'no delay expected'
For all holding instructions regardless of type
Only when holding exceeds 30 minutes
Only for non-published holding instructions

items.question_text: What does the ERAM visual indicator of SAA status display?
items.explanation: 5-13-10 provides a visual indicator of Special Activity Airspace status on the ERAM display.

choices.text: Special Activity Airspace (SAA) status
Sector activity alerts
Separation assurance alerts
System availability status

items.situation: A CA (Conflict Alert) activates on your display involving an aircraft under your control and an aircraft under another controller's jurisdiction.

choices.text: Evaluate the reason without delay. Coordinate with the other controller to ensure an effective course of action.
Only handle your own aircraft and let the other controller handle theirs
Suppress the alert and continue monitoring
Immediately vector your aircraft away without coordination

items.explanation: 5-13-1A and b: evaluate without delay and take appropriate action. If another controller is involved, initiate coordination to ensure effective course of action.
items.para_context: CONFLICT ALERT (CA) AND MODE C INTRUDER (MCI) ALERT

items.instruction: What must you enter into the computer?
items.situation: An aircraft is cleared to descend from FL350 to FL200 and maintain for a short period before further descent.
items.question_text: What altitude entry should be made?

choices.text: An interim altitude for the short-term FL200 clearance, then update when recleared
Enter the filed flight plan altitude of FL200
Enter FL350 and leave it unchanged
No entry needed; just verbal coordination

items.explanation: 5-13-3A3b: when aircraft will maintain an altitude for a short period then be recleared, enter an interim altitude.
items.para_context: COMPUTER ENTRY OF FLIGHT PLAN INFORMATION
